hero.desc: नारीसायकल (NariCycle) मासिक पाळी आरोग्य आणि प्रतिबंधात्मक आरोग्य सेवेसाठी तुमचा आरोग्य सोबती आहे.

features.title: तुमच्या आरोग्य प्रवासाला सक्षम करणे
features.desc: तुम्हाला दररोज निरोगी राहण्यास मदत करणारी स्मार्ट साधने.
features.cycle_desc: तुमच्या पुढील मासिक पाळीचा अंदाज घ्या.
features.symptoms_desc: वेदना आणि ऊर्जा पातळी ट्रॅक करा.

auth.login_subtitle: तुमच्या डॅशबोर्डवर प्रवेश करण्यासाठी साइन इन करा

chatbot.name: Nari AI Assistant
chatbot.emergency_desc: Your symptoms may require medical attention. Please consult a healthcare provider immediately.

nodes.start: Hey {{name}}! I'm your Nari health companion. How are you feeling today? Let's do a quick check-in 💕
nodes.q_whats_wrong: I'm sorry to hear that 💕 Can you tell me what's bothering you the most right now?
nodes.tip_cramps:
That sounds uncomfortable 😔 Here's something that might help:

🌿 Warm compress on your lower abdomen works wonders
☕ Ginger tea can ease muscle tension
🧘 Gentle stretching like Child's Pose helps too

Let me ask a few more questions to check on you...
nodes.tip_dizzy:
That could be important to monitor 💛 Try this right now:

🪑 Sit down if you're standing
💧 Drink water slowly
🍌 Eat something with iron — banana, dates, or jaggery

Let me check a few more things...
nodes.tip_headache:
Headaches during your cycle are common but no fun 😔

❄️ Cold compress on your forehead can help
💧 Drink water — dehydration is a top trigger
🍫 Dark chocolate has magnesium which eases headaches

Let's continue checking...
nodes.q_period: 🩸 Are you currently on your period?
nodes.q_last_cycle: 📅 No worries! When was your last period approximately?
nodes.q_cramps: 😫 Are you experiencing cramps or abdominal pain?
nodes.q_pain_severity: ⚡ I'm sorry you're dealing with that 💕 Is the pain bad enough to stop you from doing daily activities?
nodes.q_flow: 🌊 How would you describe your flow today?
nodes.q_bloating: 🫧 Are you experiencing any bloating or water retention?
nodes.q_dizzy: 😵‍💫 Do you feel dizzy, lightheaded, or unusually fatigued?
nodes.q_headache: 🤕 Any headaches or body aches today?
nodes.q_sleep: 🌙 How did you sleep last night?
nodes.q_hydration: 💧 Last one! Are you drinking enough water today?
nodes.end_normal: Great news! You seem to be doing well today. Keep staying hydrated and taking care of yourself! 🌟
nodes.end_moderate: Some of your symptoms need attention. I've prepared personalized tips below. Please monitor how you feel and consider seeing a doctor if things don't improve in 2-3 days. 💛
nodes.end_risk: Based on your symptoms, I'm concerned about your health. Please consult a healthcare provider as soon as possible. I'm sending an alert to your guardian. 💕
nodes.sos_alert: ⚠️ I've detected a critical health pattern. Notifying your emergency contact now...
nodes.fallback: Hmm, something went wrong. Let's start over! 🌸

health_tips.cramps_1: Apply a warm compress or heating pad to your lower abdomen
health_tips.cramps_2: Try gentle yoga poses like Child's Pose or Cat-Cow stretch
health_tips.cramps_3: Drink warm ginger or chamomile tea to ease muscle tension
health_tips.cramps_4: Light walking can help improve blood flow and reduce pain
health_tips.cramps_warn: If cramps are severe enough to prevent daily activities for more than 2 days, consult a doctor.
health_tips.heavy_1: Increase iron-rich foods: spinach, lentils, dates, jaggery, and beetroot
health_tips.heavy_2: Stay well hydrated — aim for 8-10 glasses of water daily
health_tips.heavy_3: Take rest and avoid intense physical activity on heavy days
health_tips.heavy_4: Consider tracking pad/tampon usage to monitor flow changes
health_tips.heavy_warn: Soaking through a pad/tampon every 1-2 hours needs immediate medical attention.
health_tips.dizzy_1: Eat small, frequent meals rich in iron and vitamin C
health_tips.dizzy_2: Include vitamin B12 sources: milk, curd, eggs, fortified cereals
health_tips.dizzy_3: Avoid standing for long periods — sit down when you feel lightheaded
health_tips.dizzy_4: Get 7-8 hours of quality sleep each night
health_tips.dizzy_warn: Frequent dizziness with pale skin or shortness of breath may indicate anaemia — see a doctor.
health_tips.pain_1: Try deep breathing exercises: inhale 4 sec, hold 4 sec, exhale 6 sec
health_tips.pain_2: A warm bath can relax tense pelvic muscles
health_tips.pain_3: Massage your lower abdomen gently in circular motions
health_tips.pain_4: Eat anti-inflammatory foods: turmeric milk, berries, green leafy vegetables
health_tips.pain_warn: Severe pelvic pain could indicate conditions like endometriosis or PCOS — please consult a gynecologist.
health_tips.headache_1: Stay hydrated — dehydration is a common headache trigger during periods
health_tips.headache_2: Apply a cold compress to your forehead or temples
health_tips.headache_3: Reduce screen time and rest in a dim, quiet room
health_tips.headache_4: Eat magnesium-rich foods: bananas, nuts, dark chocolate
health_tips.headache_warn: Persistent migraines with vision changes need medical evaluation.
health_tips.bloating_1: Reduce salt intake to minimize water retention
health_tips.bloating_2: Eat potassium-rich foods: bananas, sweet potatoes, avocados
health_tips.bloating_3: Try peppermint or fennel tea after meals
health_tips.bloating_4: Light exercise like walking helps move gas through the digestive system
health_tips.bloating_warn: Severe bloating with sharp pain could indicate something else — consult your doctor.
health_tips.sleep_1: Maintain a consistent sleep schedule — same bedtime and wake time
health_tips.sleep_2: Avoid caffeine and heavy meals 3 hours before bed
health_tips.sleep_3: Try a warm milk with turmeric (golden milk) before sleeping
health_tips.sleep_4: Keep your room cool and dark for deeper sleep
health_tips.sleep_warn: Chronic insomnia lasting more than 2 weeks should be discussed with a healthcare provider.
health_tips.hydration_1: Carry a water bottle and set hourly reminders to drink
health_tips.hydration_2: Add lemon, cucumber, or mint for flavor if plain water feels boring
health_tips.hydration_3: Eat water-rich fruits: watermelon, oranges, coconut water
health_tips.hydration_4: Drink warm water with honey first thing in the morning
